semantic.statement: Let \( f \) be continuous on a closed interval \( [a, b] \), and define \( F(x) = \int_a^x f(t)\,dt \) for \( x \in [a, b] \). Then \( F \) is continuous on \( [a, b] \), differentiable on \( (a, b) \), and \( F'(x) = f(x) \).

semantic.term: Leibniz rule for differentiation under the integral sign
semantic.statement: Let \( f \) be continuous on an interval containing the ranges of differentiable functions \( a(x) \) and \( b(x) \), and define \( \Phi(x) = \int_{a(x)}^{b(x)} f(t)\,dt \). Then \( \Phi'(x) = f(b(x))\,b'(x) - f(a(x))\,a'(x) \).
Let \( f \) be continuous on \( [a, b] \), and let \( F \) be any antiderivative of \( f \) on \( [a, b] \), so that \( F'(x) = f(x) \). Then \( \int_a^b f(x)\,dx = F(b) - F(a) \).
